title: Composite Roles
description:
## Granting Several Roles at Once

Every user who becomes a `manager` must also automatically receive the `employee` and `reports-viewer` roles — and if you later add a fourth role that managers should have, you want existing managers to pick it up **without re-touching every user**.

What is the cleanest Keycloak feature for this?

options.text: Make `manager` a **composite role** that includes `employee` and `reports-viewer`; assigning `manager` then grants the associated child roles automatically
Assign `employee` and `reports-viewer` to each manager individually, in addition to `manager`
Put all three roles in the same group and tell users to pick the right group at login
Create three separate clients, one per role, and have managers authenticate against all three

explanation: A **composite role** is a role that, when assigned, automatically confers its **associated (child) roles** — which may be realm or client roles. Making `manager` composite over `employee` and `reports-viewer` means a single `manager` assignment grants all three, and updating the composite later instantly propagates to everyone who has `manager`. (a) is correct. (b) works but is exactly the manual, error-prone duplication composites eliminate, and a future role would have to be re-applied to every user. (c) misunderstands groups — groups bundle role *assignments* for their members but users don't 'pick a group at login', and it doesn't make one role imply others. (d) is nonsensical for this purpose.

hints.title: Look for the toggle on the role itself
hints.body: In the Admin Console, open the role and enable 'Composite Roles', then add the child realm/client roles. The effective roles in a user's token will then include all the children.
